background_concepts.concept: Multispectral satellite bands (Landsat / Sentinel-2 red, NIR, SWIR)
background_concepts.why_needed: These crop and land-cover mapping papers assume the reader knows what spectral bands are, why particular satellite sensors sample red/near-infrared/short-wave-infrared, and their spatial resolution, without explaining the sensor payload.

suggested_resources.title: ESA - Introducing Sentinel-2
Sentinel 2 Bands and Combinations - GIS Geography

background_concepts.why_needed: Crop classification and drone/computer-vision pipelines rely on NDVI-type greenness indices as features but treat them as a given metric rather than explaining the red/NIR reflectance formula and its interpretation.

suggested_resources.title: Landsat Normalized Difference Vegetation Index | USGS
What Is Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI)?

background_concepts.concept: Solar-Induced Fluorescence (SIF) as a photosynthesis proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: The super-resolution SIF paper assumes the reader knows that SIF is a faint re-emitted signal from chlorophyll during photosynthesis and serves as a proxy for gross primary productivity, without teaching the underlying physical process.

suggested_resources.title: Solar Induced Fluorescence: Learn a New Approach to Remote Sensing of Vegetation | NASA Applied Sciences
NASA ARSET SIF Primer Slides (Vegetation Photosynthesis)

background_concepts.concept: Vegetation greenness indices (NDVI) for health/yield prediction
background_concepts.why_needed: The vegetation health prediction pipeline uses NDVI-based greenness as its target variable without introducing what the index measures or its value range.

suggested_resources.title: Landsat Normalized Difference Vegetation Index | USGS

domain_name: Digital Agricultural Extension & Farmer Information Systems

background_concepts.concept: Interactive Voice Response (IVR) and voice-forum platforms for low-literacy users
background_concepts.why_needed: These extension papers deploy IVR/voice-forum and DVD-based participatory systems as the delivery channel, assuming familiarity with how automated telephony menus and voice social networks work in low-literacy rural contexts.

suggested_resources.title: Interactive voice response - Wikipedia (ICTD / developing countries section)
What Is Interactive Voice Response (IVR)? | IBM

background_concepts.concept: Mandi / APMC regulated market system and e-NAM
background_concepts.why_needed: The Mobile Mandi and market-intelligence papers assume the reader knows the Indian mandi/APMC institutional structure, the role of commission agents/intermediaries, and the e-NAM electronic trading portal, which shape how prices are discovered.

suggested_resources.title: Agricultural Produce Market Committee (APMC) | Indian Economic Service - Arthapedia
Understanding the Agricultural Produce Market Committee (APMC) System

background_concepts.concept: Conservation tillage and crop residue management
background_concepts.why_needed: The residue-density segmentation paper assumes the reader understands why measuring surface crop residue matters for tillage practices, erosion control, and soil carbon, without explaining the agronomic rationale.

suggested_resources.title: Soil Carbon Sequestration—Fundamentals | Ohioline (Ohio State)

background_concepts.concept: Sentinel-2 30m surface reflectance and revisit cadence
background_concepts.why_needed: The irrigation-detection and IrrMap datasets rely on Sentinel-2 imagery, spatial resolution and revisit frequency as the measurement substrate without introducing the mission's sensing characteristics.

suggested_resources.title: USGS EROS Archive - Sentinel-2 | U.S. Geological Survey

domain_name: In-field IoT and sensing for precision agriculture

background_concepts.concept: Vegetation/soil-moisture remote sensing indices used as sensing signals
background_concepts.why_needed: The soil-moisture forecasting papers combine in-ground sensors with Earth-observation greenness/reflectance features and assume familiarity with satellite-derived indices as inputs alongside physical sensors.

suggested_resources.title: What Is Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI)?

domain_name: Agricultural supply chain intermediaries and procurement mechanisms

background_concepts.concept: Incentive compatibility and mechanism design (revelation principle, truthful mechanisms)
background_concepts.why_needed: The incentive-compatible procurement paper is built on mechanism-design terminology (dominant-strategy incentive compatibility, truthful/allocation-payment rules) that it uses without defining.

suggested_resources.title: Foundations of Mechanism Design: A Tutorial (Y. Narahari, IISc)
Introduction to Mechanism Design (Wasser, Micro II lecture notes)

domain_name: Human-centered design of low-cost agricultural tools and machines
Climate-smart farming practices and carbon/emissions in agriculture

background_concepts.why_needed: The soil-carbon RL optimization and carbon-smart farming papers assume the reader understands how management practices transfer atmospheric CO2 into soil organic matter and why this is a climate mitigation lever, without teaching the process.

suggested_resources.title: Soil Organic Carbon in Temperate Managed Ecosystems: A Primer (USDA Climate Hubs)
Soil Carbon Sequestration—Fundamentals | Ohioline

background_concepts.concept: Reinforcement learning (agent, state, action, reward, policy)
background_concepts.why_needed: The crop-management and soil-carbon optimization papers frame farm decisions as an RL/MDP problem and assume familiarity with the agent-environment loop and reward maximization.

suggested_resources.title: Part 1: Key Concepts in RL — OpenAI Spinning Up
🤗 Deep Reinforcement Learning Course - Introduction

domain_name: Water and irrigation infrastructure for food/water security
Alternative and diversified livelihood cultivation systems for smallholders
